cases.title: Testing-plan §7.3 and §11 NFR-09 row — six-role authorization regression with no cross-scope leakage
preconditions: Testing-plan §7.3 security and authorization tests and §11 NFR verification matrix row for NFR-09 apply.
role-matrix fixture provides authenticated users for Student, Lecturer, DepartmentAdmin, AcademicAdmin, ITAdmin, and SystemAuditor with known scope boundaries.
Attendance, session, audit, and export data span multiple class sections and faculties to exercise scope isolation.
steps: For each role, exercise allowed protected routes per permissions matrix §3.1–3.2: reports, exports, audit logs, session control, and attendance reads.

For each role, attempt denied actions: Student on audit logs and exports; Lecturer on unassigned section exports and session control; ITAdmin on academic exports without grant; SystemAuditor on mutations and exports.
Verify GET /v1/reports/attendance and POST /v1/exports/attendance enforce role scope per BR-18 and BR-19.
Verify GET /v1/audit-logs is limited to AcademicAdmin, scoped ITAdmin technical context, scoped Lecturer limited events, and SystemAuditor read-only scope.
Inspect every denial response for restricted data leakage.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §7.3 and §11 NFR-09 minimum evidence: only authorized scope is accessible for each of the six roles; every unauthorized protected route or export operation is denied without cross-scope data leakage (AC-23, NFR-09, RBAC-01).

cases.title: M01 authorize enforces deny-by-default at module boundary before repository queries
preconditions: Integration database seeded with role-to-scope assignments and attendance rows across multiple sections and faculties.
M01 Identity and Access authorize(action, resource, scopeContext) uses real DB lookups for actor role and target entity scope.
Report, export, and audit query services invoke M01 before unrestricted repository access.
steps: Invoke M01 authorize for Student with AuditLog.read; assert Deny.
Invoke M01 authorize for Lecturer with ExportJob.execute targeting out-of-scope classSectionId; assert Deny.
Invoke M01 authorize for ITAdmin with ReportView.read without academic grant; assert Deny.
Invoke export job creation use-case for Lecturer with foreign classSectionId filter and confirm scope guard short-circuits before export query.
Assert zero rows from foreign scope are read, persisted, or returned.
cases.expected: M01 module boundary rejects all unauthorized operations with deny-by-default semantics (RBAC-01, RBAC-02, PRM-10); no cross-scope attendance, audit, or export data is accessed before authorization passes.

cases.title: Export scope filter module rejects out-of-scope lecturer export at DB transaction boundary
preconditions: Integration database contains attendance rows in section A (lecturer assigned) and section B (lecturer not assigned).
Export service uses real DB transaction and scope-resolution module per PRM-09.
Lecturer actor is authenticated with assignment set limited to section A only.
steps: Invoke export job creation use-case for Lecturer with filters { classSectionId: sectionBId, format: csv }.
Verify scope guard rejects the operation before attendance query executes.
Query export_jobs table for new rows tied to the denied request.
Invoke export job creation for Lecturer with filters { classSectionId: sectionAId } as positive control.
cases.expected: Out-of-scope export is rejected at module boundary with no export_jobs row persisted; in-scope export control succeeds with scope-filtered dataset only (PRM-01, PRM-09, BR-18).

cases.title: HTTP contract for Student denied on GET /v1/audit-logs returns 403
preconditions: Student user is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Audit log entries exist for attendance mutations and exports across institution scope.
steps: Send GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=Export&page=1&pageSize=25 with Student Authorization header.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope per API §2.3.
Inspect data field, meta.pagination, and error.code value.
Verify no audit log items, actor identifiers, or export scope metadata are disclosed.
cases.expected: Response is 403 with standard { data: null, meta, error } envelope; error.code is Forbidden or OutOfScope; data is null and no audit entries or restricted scope metadata are disclosed (AuditLog.read Deny for Student per API §4.7).

cases.title: HTTP contract for Lecturer denied on POST /v1/exports/attendance for unassigned classSectionId
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned only to section A.
Section B has attendance data and is not in lecturer assignment set.
Attendance export data exists for both sections.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with body { format: csv, filters: { classSectionId: sectionBId } } and required Idempotency-Key as Lecturer.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope.
Verify no exportJobId is returned and no export job is created in persistence.
Inspect error.details for restricted section B row samples or student identifiers.
cases.expected: Response is 403 with standard envelope; error.code is Forbidden or OutOfScope; data is null with no exportJobId, queued job metadata, or section B attendance data disclosed (ExportJob.execute scoped denial per BR-19).

cases.title: HTTP contract for unauthenticated GET /v1/reports/attendance returns 401
preconditions: Institution-wide attendance report data exists in the database.
No Authorization header is supplied on the request.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 without Bearer token.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope per API §6.1 pipeline step 1.
Inspect data field and error.code value.
cases.expected: Response is 401 with standard { data: null, meta, error } envelope; error.code is Unauthenticated; data is null and no attendance rows or scope metadata are disclosed before role evaluation.

cases.title: Student denied GET /v1/audit-logs without audit entry or actor metadata leakage
preconditions: Student is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
dispute-trail fixture includes manual corrections, export audit entries, and failed check-in attempts across multiple sections.
steps: Call GET /v1/audit-logs?targetId={studentUserId}&classSessionId={sessionId} as Student.
Call GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=attendance_status_mutation&from={start}&to={end} as Student.
Call GET /v1/audit-logs with no filters as Student.
Inspect each response for HTTP 403, error.code, and absence of audit payloads.
cases.expected: All audit log queries return 403 Forbidden; responses contain no audit log items, mutation old/new values, export scope filters, lecturer actor identifiers, or other students' dispute evidence (permissions matrix §3.2 AuditLog.read Deny for Student).

cases.title: Lecturer denied cross-section report query and export for unassigned classSectionId
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned only to section A.
Section B has attendance data and closed sessions; section B is not in lecturer assignment set.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?classSectionId={sectionBId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as Lecturer.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters { classSectionId: sectionBId, format: csv } as Lecturer.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sectionBSessionId}/attendance as Lecturer.
Inspect each denial response for partial section B data leakage.
cases.expected: All three requests return 403 with OutOfScope or Forbidden semantics; responses contain no partial section B rows, attendance statuses, student identifiers, roster counts, or export job identifiers (PRM-01, PRM-10).

cases.title: ITAdmin denied institution-wide academic report and export without explicit elevated grant
preconditions: ITAdmin is authenticated without explicit academic access grant.
Attendance data exists across institution scope.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId} as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with term-wide csv filters as ITAdmin.
Call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as ITAdmin.
Verify ITAdmin can still access technical audit context via GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=login_lock if within technical scope.
cases.expected: Academic report, export, and roster requests return 403 Forbidden with no attendance rows, pagination totals, or export job artifacts; technical audit access remains limited to ITAdmin technical context per permissions matrix §5.5 (RBAC-03).

cases.title: Student denied institution-wide report and export execution
preconditions: Student is authenticated.
Institution-wide attendance data exists across multiple sections including sections the student is not enrolled in.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId} as Student.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv and term-wide filters as Student.
Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?studentUserId={otherStudentId} as Student attempting horizontal privilege escalation.
cases.expected: Institution-wide report and export return 403 Forbidden; studentUserId override for another student is denied; responses contain no foreign student attendance rows, pagination totals, or exportJobId (ReportView.read self-only, ExportJob.execute Deny for Student).

cases.title: Denied authorization responses do not leak cross-scope pagination or entity metadata
preconditions: Institution has 500+ attendance rows across sections; unauthorized actor lacks scope for requested filters.
Unauthorized Student or cross-scope Lecturer is authenticated.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as unauthorized actor.
Inspect error response body, meta.pagination fields, and error.details if present.
Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with foreign classSectionId filter as scoped lecturer.
Send GET /v1/audit-logs as Student and inspect denial envelope.
Compare institution-wide totals against any values returned in denial responses.
cases.expected: All denials return 403 without meta.pagination.totalItems or totalPages reflecting institution-wide or foreign-scope counts; error.details contains no student names, attendance statuses, audit payloads, or section row samples (PRM-10, CMP-04).

cases.title: Authorized Lecturer workflow confines session control, roster, and export to assigned scope
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned to section SE101-01 with scheduled session and enrolled students.
Attendance data exists in SE101-01 and unassigned section SE101-02.
steps: Authenticate as lecturer; POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open.
GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and verify roster rows are section-scoped.
POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and filters covering assigned section only.
Verify export output contains only SE101-01 rows.
Attempt GET /v1/reports/attendance?classSectionId={SE101-02Id} and confirm denial.
cases.expected: End-to-end authorized workflow completes within lecturer assigned scope with no SE101-02 leakage; out-of-scope read attempt is denied, confirming positive and negative NFR-09 enforcement (AC-15, PRM-01).

cases.title: Scope guard prevents paginated report query from enumerating foreign-section rows
preconditions: Integration DB has multi-section attendance data; actor scope covers zero rows for requested filter.
Report repository uses real pagination query with page and pageSize parameters.
steps: Execute report list query as unauthorized Lecturer with foreign classSectionId filter, page=1, pageSize=25.
Measure whether any DB read occurs against foreign-section attendance tables before denial.
Verify denial occurs without returning empty data array paired with institution-wide totalItems.
Repeat with page=2 to confirm no partial foreign data on later pages.
cases.expected: Authorization failure returns before unrestricted paginated scan; no meta.pagination totals from foreign scope are computed or returned on any page boundary.

cases.title: Student browser journey blocked from audit log listing and detail surfaces
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile or desktop browser.
PG-15 /audit/logs route exists in application shell.
dispute-trail fixture provides audit entries for mutations and exports that Student must not access.
steps: Observe main navigation menu for audit log links.
Attempt direct URL navigation to /audit/logs.
If audit page is reachable, observe table content and filter controls.
Verify access-denied state, redirect, or empty safe messaging without audit entry rows.
cases.expected: Student cannot access audit log listing or detail UI; direct URL navigation shows access-denied state or redirect without displaying mutation trails, export scope metadata, or other actors' audit evidence (AuditLog.read Deny for Student).

cases.title: Lecturer browser journey gates export section filter to assigned scope and denies foreign-section tamper
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser.
Reporting and export UI section filter is populated from lecturer assignment set only.
Attendance data exists in assigned section A and unassigned section B.
Deep-link or URL tamper to foreign classSectionId is possible.
steps: Navigate to attendance report or export page.
Confirm section selector lists only assigned sections.
Trigger export for an assigned section and verify download or job confirmation.
Attempt export with URL or form parameter set to unassigned section B classSectionId.
Observe UI error state and network response for export request.
cases.expected: UI does not list unassigned sections; assigned-section export succeeds with scoped output; tampered foreign-section export request shows access-denied feedback without previewing section B attendance rows or partial export download.
